data.title: Meet Slate Auto: The Jeff Bezos-backed startup looking to disrupt the electric truck market
data.description:
Slate Auto is a Michigan-based electric vehicle (EV) startup backed by Jeff Bezos, aiming to produce an affordable two-seat electric pickup truck priced around $25,000. Founded in 2022 as part of Re:Build Manufacturing, the company has been growning rapidly. 

Notable investors include Bezos, Mark Walter, the controlling owner of the LA Dodgers and CEO of Guggenheim Partners, and Thomas Tull, who is a lead investor of Re:Build Manufacturing. The Series A funding round brought in $111 million.

The company's strategy is focused on creating an affordable truck designed for first-time buyers. Inspired by iconic vehicles like the Ford Model T and Volkswagen Beetle, Slate plans to enhance profitability through customization and accessories under the slogan "We built it. You make it".

Slate Auto is headquartered in Troy, Michigan, with a design studio in Long Beach, California, and plans for a factory in Indiana. The startup has been operating in stealth mode, although it recently showcased a prototype of its pickup truck to investors. The vehicle emphasizes simplicity and practicality, aiming to appeal to a broad market while leveraging open-source software for customization

Indiana lobbying records indicate that Slate Auto aims to start vehicle production as soon as late 2026 at a manufacturing facility somewhere near Indianapolis, Indiana.

Slate Auto could be a future competitor in the electric truck market with Ford Motor (F), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), and Chevrolet (GM).

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow advance as Trump spares tech sector from retaliatory tariffs
data.description:
Traders and investors on Wall Street push the major market averages higher on Monday as President Donald Trump announced temporary tariff exemptions on smartphones, computers, and other electronics primarily imported from China.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was higher by 0.3%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has climbed up 0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was in the green by 0.3%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, all 11 S&P sectors have moved up into positive territory with Info Tech and Communication Services leading the way. At the other end, Health Care has been the weakest performer on the day. 

Shifting to the Treasury market and yields are lower. The longer end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 8 basis points to 4.41%. At the same time, the shorter end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell 8 basis points to 3.89%. 

Wall Street’s positive momentum push has been fueled by Trump's announcement that tariffs were exempt for smartphones, computers, and other electronics mostly imported from China, providing major relief for American tech players. 

"Tariffs continued to dominate headlines over the weekend, starting with news late on Friday that smartphones, computers, and some other electronics would be exempt from U.S. reciprocal tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said. 

"Over the weekend, Trump partially retreated from the retreat, emphasizing that a 20% tax would apply and additional unspecified taxes on technology are coming. This erratic policy making leads to investors questioning the existence of a competent plan," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

The upcoming week is set to feature a wave of major earnings reports from top companies, including Bank of America (BAC), Netflix (NFLX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and American Express (AXP).

On the economic front, U.S. consumers have slightly tempered their inflation expectations for the long-term even as they hike their projections on short-term inflation, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's March Survey of Consumer Expectations released on Monday.

Additionally, Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic and Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin make remarks.

As for stocks that are on the move, Palantir pops 4.5% after landing a deal to supply NATO with AI-powered battle system.

data.title: We are 'nowhere near the end' of the current economic storm
data.description:
The U.S. economy is "nowhere near the end of the storm" amid shifting U.S. policies, said Win Thin, Brown Brothers Harriman’s head of currency strategy.

During a CNBC interview, Thin argued that the “U.S. exceptionalism story has really been upended” as growth-friendly deregulation and tax cuts gave way to tariffs, deportations, and DOGE-related layoffs under the current administration.

He said these policy changes have contributed significantly to market uncertainty.

Thin said he said observation “strange price action” in markets, with the dollar weakening and Treasury yields rising despite typical risk-off conditions.

While he noted solid demand at recent U.S. Treasury auctions, Thin shared his concerns about a “loss in confidence in U.S. assets” due to unpredictable policymaking. “Markets like certainty, they hate uncertainty,” he said.

In addition, policy opacity has left the Federal Reserve in a “wait and see mode,” according to Thin.

He described the dollar’s decline as “cyclical” and “man-made,” tied to three months of destabilizing measures. He said that regaining investor trust will require time and clearer economic direction from Washington.

Thin concluded that the U.S. faces a prolonged period of turbulence, telling CNBC that “we’re in the early days” of navigating these challenges.

Market participants now confront a landscape where traditional indicators like dollar strength and yield movements defy historical patterns, reflecting a “strange milieu” of disrupted expectations, he said.

data.title: Neurocrine upgraded at RBC Capital Markets on recent selloff
data.description:
• Shares of Neurocrine Biosciences (NASDAQ:NBIX) gained on Monday after RBC Capital Markets upgraded the maker of movement disorder therapy Ingrezza, citing an attractive entry point given the recent market selloff.
  • Broader sector selloff and market downside “has brought the stock to levels well below fundamental fair value, given an Ingrezza commercial franchise that we expect to be relatively solid and durable long term,” RBC analyst Brian Abrahams wrote.
  • Upgrading the stock to Outperform from Sector Perform, Abrahams, however, adjusted his per-share target to $137 from $138, expecting volatility linked to the company’s Q1 results and its long-term exposure to Medicare drug price negotiations.
  • The analyst added that Neurocrine’s (NASDAQ:NBIX) tariff-related exposure could be low relative to its rivals and noted that its sub-$100 share price “provides a good entry point for a stable, safe mid-cap biotech.”

data.title: Fate Therapeutics gets RMAT status from FDA for Lupus treatment
data.description:
Fate Therapeutics (NASDAQ:FATE) announced that the U.S. FDA granted Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy designation to FT819 for the treatment of lupus.

FT819 is an experimental treatment currently being tested in a Phase 1 clinical trial. It’s a type of CAR T-cell therapy, being developed to treat systemic lupus erythematosus, a chronic autoimmune disease where the immune system attacks healthy tissues.

Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy designation entails benefits such as early interactions with the FDA to discuss surrogate or intermediate endpoints that could support the accelerated approval and meet post-approval requirements. It can also allow companies to win the priority review for a biologics license application.

The company’s RMAT  application included initial safety and activity data from patients treated with FT819 in an ongoing early stage trial. 

The stock is however down 13%.

data.title: Yellen says recent bond selloff signals 'loss of confidence' in U.S. policymaking
data.description:
Former U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen on Monday attributed the recent, unusual bond-market swoon to a "loss of confidence in U.S. economic policy," although “I don’t think we’re seeing dysfunction — in the sense of liquidity completely drying up in the markets."

Stoking the drop in consumer sentiment, President Donald Trump is "pounding the U.S. economy with a sledgehammer" with his unpredictable tariff policies, Yellen, who left office in January, told CNBC in a virtual interview. 

In any case, the pattern of rising Treasury yields and a weaker dollar "is really very worrisome," she said. This trend likely indicates "investors are beginning to shun dollar-based assets," raising doubts about the security of government-backed U.S. Treasuries amid fears of higher inflation and recession.

All else equal, higher yields attract foreign capital, boosting demand for dollars, while lower yields reduce that demand, weakening the dollar. But the textbooks weren't prepared for the disruptive tariff regime.

Should financial stability risks emerge, the former Fed chair said, the central bank is equipped to intervene. “The tariff policies, and the uncertainty they’re creating, create the most difficult possible situation for the Fed.” 

Yellen does "not yet see those [risks] in play," noting she was happy with last week's strong Treasury auctions for longer-dated debt.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: FTC trial forcing Meta to divest WhatsApp and Instagram set to begin
data.description:
Starting today, Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) will face a Federal Trade Commission trial that is aiming to undo its acquisition of WhatsApp and Instagram from a decade ago.

The U.S. agency filed the case against the company in December 2020, towards the end of Donald Trump's first presidential term, alleging that the Facebook owner then bought the messaging platform and the photo messaging app to crush competition.

The FTC is looking to pressure the social media and tech giant to restructure or divest its business. Meta said in a blog post Sunday that the FTC lawsuit is "absurd" and also threw shade at the Trump-Vance administration for its heightened interest in TikTok U.S. (BDNCE).

"It’s absurd that the FTC is trying to break up a great American company at the same time the Administration is trying to save Chinese-owned TikTok. And, it makes no sense for regulators to try and weaken U.S. companies right at the moment we most need them to invest in winning the competition with China for leadership in AI," Meta said Sunday.

"The evidence at trial will show what every 17-year-old in the world knows: Instagram competes with TikTok (and YouTube and X and many other apps)," Meta said.

Earlier this month, top boss Mark Zuckerberg tried to lobby senior Trump officials to agree to a settlement that would prevent his company from facing the FTC trial. He has made three trips to the White House since Trump's second term commenced.

Joe Simonson, an FTC spokesperson, said, "The Trump-Vance FTC could not be more ready for this trial." 

"Despite what you might hear, this case isn’t about censorship or content decisions. In fact, the word “censorship” doesn’t appear anywhere in the FTC’s briefs. This case is about the reality of the competition we face today," the company added.

The FTC trial will be presided over by U.S. District Judge James Boasberg and is expected to go on for seven to eight weeks.

Mark Zuckerberg, former COO Sheryl Sandberg, and the head of Instagram, Adam Mosseri, are expected to testify in the trial.

data.title: Southern California Edison outlines $925M rebuilding plan after L.A. wildfires
data.description:
Edison International (NYSE:EIX) +1.9% in Monday's trading after saying late Friday it had submitted an initial plan to rebuild the Altadena and Malibu areas that were devastated by January's wildfires, including the replacement of more than 150 miles of damaged electrical power lines with underground lines.

Edison (NYSE:EIX) subsidiary Southern California Edison said it submitted the preliminary plan to California's Governor Newsom, and that the years-long project would cost in the $860M-$925M range while ultimately reducing the future risk of wildfire in the devastated communities.

The rebuilding plan includes undergrounding 40 circuit miles of distribution lines in Altadena and surrounding neighborhoods that have ben determined to be high fire risk areas and 23 circuit miles in non-high risk areas, along with 90 circuit miles in Malibu and the nearby high fire risk areas, and the company is evaluating the feasibility of an additional 19 circuit miles in Altadena.

Although no official cause for the fires has been released, several lawsuits have claimed that SoCal Edison power lines and towers in Altadena started the Eaton Fire, and Edison (EIX) has acknowledged the possibility of its equipment being involved in the cause of the Eaton Fire.

"SCE will build back a resilient, reliable grid for our customers," SoCal Edison CEO Steven Powell said. "We are increasing electrical capacity in fire-impacted communities and exploring advanced technologies, such as battery backup for critical care customers and smart panels."

data.title: Thomasville Bancshares GAAP EPS of $1.58
data.description:
• Thomasville Bancshares  press release (OTCPK:THVB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.58.
  • YTD Return on Average Assets of 2.27% and Return on Average Tangible Equity of 24.27%.
  • Total Assets of $1.899 billion, an increase of $156 million over the same period in 2024.
  • Loans grew to $1.592 billion, an increase of $158 million or 11% year-over-year.
  • Deposits grew to $1.642 billion, an increase of $134 million or 9% year-over-year.
